text:
codebasics.io 
 
5 AI Projects That will Matter in 2026 
 
1. RAG with RBAC, Guardrails and Monitoring 
 
RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) is widely used in AI projects worldwide. In our AI 
consultancy company AtliQ, majority of AI implementations we have worked on for our 
clients based in USA and UAE are RAG projects.  
LLMs have knowledge cut-off date, for example GPT 5.4 is trained on data till Aug 2025. If

LLMs have knowledge cut-off date, for example GPT 5.4 is trained on data till Aug 2025. If 
you want to build AI application that uses GPT 5.4 as a brain and reasons over some private 
organizational data or public information post Aug 2025, you will have to use RAG.  
What you will build 
• Build internal chatbot for a company that can answer questions using company’s 
private data 
• Limit the access of information using Role Based Access Control (RBAC) which

private data 
• Limit the access of information using Role Based Access Control (RBAC) which 
means only finance team gets access to financial reports, marketing expenses. Only 
HR team can access employee data and payroll. Only C-level executives have full 
access to all company data 
• Setup guardrails: Protect sensitive / PII data, Out-of-scope question detection 
• Deploy in Azure. Setup monitoring and evaluation so that whenever a new change

• Deploy in Azure. Setup monitoring and evaluation so that whenever a new change 
is deployed it will run evals to make sure things don’t break.  
• Cost monitoring: Setup tracking for token cost, raise alerts when needed 
Tech Stack 
• Core framework: Langchain and/or Docling along with Python  
• Vector Database: Any vector store such as Qdrant, Chromadb or Milvus 
• Frontend: Streamlit, or vibe code react web app using Claude Code 
• LLM: GPT-OSS or Llama (Use it in groq cloud for free)

• LLM: GPT-OSS or Llama (Use it in groq cloud for free) 
• Cloud: Azure or AWS or GCP 
• Evaluation and Monitoring: Ragas, Langsmith 
 
codebasics.io 
 
 
Resources 
• Download problem statement, datafiles etc. from here: 
https://codebasics.io/challenges/codebasics-gen-ai-data-science-resume-project-
challenge/19 
• Vectordb explained: https://youtu.be/72XgD322wZ8?si=8qpIwk5ZpOG-Rms7 
• Groqcloud: https://console.groq.com/ 
• Ragas: https://docs.ragas.io/en/stable/

• Groqcloud: https://console.groq.com/ 
• Ragas: https://docs.ragas.io/en/stable/ 
• LangSmith: https://smith.langchain.com/ 
• RAG in Langchain: https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/rag 
• Guardrails in Langchain: 
https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langchain/guardrails 
 
2. Voice Agent 
 
Customer service phone calls can be automated by building voice agents. 
What you will build 
• Voice agent should be able to listen to a caller, convert speech to text, understand

• Voice agent should be able to listen to a caller, convert speech to text, understand 
the customer’s intent using an LLM, retrieve relevant information from company 
knowledge bases (using RAG), and respond back in natural speech using text-to-
speech.  
• Such an agent can answer FAQs, check order status, schedule appointments, or 
route complex issues to a human agent. 
codebasics.io 
 
Tech Stack 
• Voice platform (Commercial): Elevenlabs (their free plan has 10K free credits per

Tech Stack 
• Voice platform (Commercial): Elevenlabs (their free plan has 10K free credits per 
month) or Vapi 
• Voice platform (opensource): Livekit 
• Core framework: Python with LangChain / LangGraph for orchestration and agent 
flow 
• Telephony: Twilio to buy a phone number and manage inbound / outbound calls 
• Vector Database: Any vector store such as Qdrant, ChromaDB, or Milvus for 
knowledge retrieval 
• Frontend: Streamlit dashboard, or vibe-code React web app using Claude Code

knowledge retrieval 
• Frontend: Streamlit dashboard, or vibe-code React web app using Claude Code 
• LLM: GPT-OSS or Llama for reasoning and response generation, hosted on Groq 
Cloud for free where applicable 
• STT (Speech to text) providers: Deepgram, Cartesia 
• TTS (Text to speech) providers: Elevanlabs, Cartesia, vapi 
• Cloud: Azure or AWS or GCP 
   
 
codebasics.io 
 
 
Resources 
• Build voice agent with Elevenlabs: 
https://youtu.be/fnivYSh0Cqk?si=kJgfXEjnMuyy46pT

Resources 
• Build voice agent with Elevenlabs: 
https://youtu.be/fnivYSh0Cqk?si=kJgfXEjnMuyy46pT 
• Vapi: https://vapi.ai/ 
• Elevanlabs: https://elevenlabs.io/ 
• Twillio: https://www.twilio.com/ 
3. Multi-Agent Coding Assistant 
 
Modern AI systems are moving toward agentic software development, where AI behaves 
like a small engineering team instead of a single chatbot. In this project you will build an AI

like a small engineering team instead of a single chatbot. In this project you will build an AI 
coding assistant that can take a natural language request and generate a working software 
project automatically. 
The idea is inspired by the Coder Buddy project, an AI-powered coding assistant built with 
LangGraph that behaves like a multi-agent development team capable of converting a 
prompt into a working project file-by-file.  
For example, a user could type:

prompt into a working project file-by-file.  
For example, a user could type: 
“Build a calculator web app with add, subtract, multiply and divide buttons.” 
codebasics.io 
 
The system will automatically plan the architecture, create required files, and generate 
code for the application. 
 
What you will build 
• Build an AI coding assistant that converts natural language requests into working 
code projects 
• Implement a Planner Agent that analyzes the user request and creates a project

code projects 
• Implement a Planner Agent that analyzes the user request and creates a project 
plan 
• Implement an Architect Agent that breaks the plan into specific engineering tasks 
for each file 
• Implement a Coder Agent that generates code and writes it directly into project files 
• Allow the system to generate multi-file applications such as web apps or APIs 
• Provide an interface where users can download the generated project or run it 
locally 
Tech Stack

locally 
Tech Stack 
• Agent framework: LangGraph or CrewAI with Python 
• Frontend: Streamlit, or vibe code react web app using Claude Code 
• LLM: Kimi K2, Qwen 3 (Use it in groq cloud for free) 
Resources 
• Guided YouTube Tutorial: https://youtu.be/SP-b_G74Nuk?si=HBvgIOa9nBbayGdT 
4. Multimodal AI assistant 
 
Build a multimodal AI assistant where user can attach image and AI will assist with the 
queries. Many finance and operations teams often see a charge in a credit card statement,

queries. Many finance and operations teams often see a charge in a credit card statement, 
invoice, or expense report and want a quick explanation. In this project you will build an AI 
assistant where a user uploads a financial document screenshot or PDF and asks a 
question like “Why was this charge deducted?” 
 
codebasics.io 
 
 
codebasics.io 
 
The system uses vision + document extraction + retrieval + reasoning to understand the 
document and explain the charge. 
What You’ll build

document and explain the charge. 
What You’ll build 
• Upload a screenshot of a credit card statement, invoice, receipt or expense 
report 
• Extract fields such as vendor name, line item, subtotal, tax, total amount and 
billing date 
• Use a vision-capable LLM to understand the document layout and identify the 
charge 
• Retrieve supporting context such as prior invoices or billing policies using a vector 
database 
• Generate a grounded answer explaining the reason for the charge

database 
• Generate a grounded answer explaining the reason for the charge 
• Deploy the application in cloud and monitor performance, accuracy and cost 
Tech Stack 
• Core framework: Langchain and/or Docling along with Python 
• Vector Database: Any vector store such as Qdrant, Chromadb or Milvus 
• Frontend: Streamlit, or vibe code react web app using Claude Code 
• LLM: GPT-OSS or Llama (Use it in groq cloud for free) 
• Cloud: Azure or AWS or GCP

• LLM: GPT-OSS or Llama (Use it in groq cloud for free) 
• Cloud: Azure or AWS or GCP 
• Evaluation and Monitoring: Ragas, Langsmith 
Resources 
• Docling: https://docling-project.github.io/docling/concepts/chunking/ 
 
5. Text Classification using Regex + Statistical ML + LLM 
 
Text classification remains a fundamental need for many AI applications. In this project, 
you will build a hybrid classification model using regular expression, statistical ml and LLM 
to classify programming logs.

to classify programming logs.  
codebasics.io 
 
 
 
What You’ll build 
• Classify programming logs using a hybrid approach 
• Text that has fixed patterns will be classified using regular expression 
• Text that has variable patterns but enough labeled data will be classified using BERT 
and LogisticRegression 
• Text that has complex patterns with very few labeled data points will be classified 
using LLM  
codebasics.io 
 
Tech Stack 
• Core framework: Python, Sklearn, Pandas, Groq

using LLM  
codebasics.io 
 
Tech Stack 
• Core framework: Python, Sklearn, Pandas, Groq 
• Frontend: Streamlit 
• Backend: FastAPI 
• LLM: Deepseek or Qwen 
Resources 
• YouTube tutorial: https://youtu.be/Ji3_VX80YJg?si=fBcRtROakKSuNp2u 
• Additional Dataset: https://www.kaggle.com/search?q=text+classification

• Deploy in Azure. Setup monitoring and evaluation so that whenever a new change

• Deploy in Azure. Setup monitoring and evaluation so that whenever a new change 
is deployed it will run evals to make sure things don’t break.  
• Cost monitoring: Setup tracking for token cost, raise alerts when needed

2. Voice Agent 
 
Customer service phone calls can be automated by building voice agents. 
What you will build 
• Voice agent should be able to listen to a caller, convert speech to text, understand

• Voice agent should be able to listen to a caller, convert speech to text, understand 
the customer’s intent using an LLM, retrieve relevant information from company 
knowledge bases (using RAG), and respond back in natural speech using text-to-
speech.  
• Such an agent can answer FAQs, check order status, schedule appointments, or 
route complex issues to a human agent.

3. Multi-Agent Coding Assistant 
 
Modern AI systems are moving toward agentic software development, where AI behaves 
like a small engineering team instead of a single chatbot. In this project you will build an AI

like a small engineering team instead of a single chatbot. In this project you will build an AI 
coding assistant that can take a natural language request and generate a working software 
project automatically. 
The idea is inspired by the Coder Buddy project, an AI-powered coding assistant built with 
LangGraph that behaves like a multi-agent development team capable of converting a 
prompt into a working project file-by-file.  
For example, a user could type:

prompt into a working project file-by-file.  
For example, a user could type: 
“Build a calculator web app with add, subtract, multiply and divide buttons.” 
codebasics.io 
 
The system will automatically plan the architecture, create required files, and generate 
code for the application. 
 
What you will build 
• Build an AI coding assistant that converts natural language requests into working 
code projects 
• Implement a Planner Agent that analyzes the user request and creates a project

code projects 
• Implement a Planner Agent that analyzes the user request and creates a project 
plan 
• Implement an Architect Agent that breaks the plan into specific engineering tasks 
for each file 
• Implement a Coder Agent that generates code and writes it directly into project files 
• Allow the system to generate multi-file applications such as web apps or APIs 
• Provide an interface where users can download the generated project or run it 
locally 
Tech Stack

locally 
Tech Stack 
• Agent framework: LangGraph or CrewAI with Python 
• Frontend: Streamlit, or vibe code react web app using Claude Code 
• LLM: Kimi K2, Qwen 3 (Use it in groq cloud for free)

4. Multimodal AI assistant 
 
Build a multimodal AI assistant where user can attach image and AI will assist with the 
queries. Many finance and operations teams often see a charge in a credit card statement,

queries. Many finance and operations teams often see a charge in a credit card statement, 
invoice, or expense report and want a quick explanation. In this project you will build an AI 
assistant where a user uploads a financial document screenshot or PDF and asks a 
question like “Why was this charge deducted?” 
 
codebasics.io 
 
 
codebasics.io 
 
The system uses vision + document extraction + retrieval + reasoning to understand the 
document and explain the charge. 
What You’ll build

document and explain the charge. 
What You’ll build 
• Upload a screenshot of a credit card statement, invoice, receipt or expense 
report 
• Extract fields such as vendor name, line item, subtotal, tax, total amount and 
billing date 
• Use a vision-capable LLM to understand the document layout and identify the 
charge 
• Retrieve supporting context such as prior invoices or billing policies using a vector 
database 
• Generate a grounded answer explaining the reason for the charge

database 
• Generate a grounded answer explaining the reason for the charge 
• Deploy the application in cloud and monitor performance, accuracy and cost

5. Text Classification using Regex + Statistical ML + LLM 
 
Text classification remains a fundamental need for many AI applications. In this project, 
you will build a hybrid classification model using regular expression, statistical ml and LLM 
to classify programming logs.

to classify programming logs.  
codebasics.io 
 
 
 
What You’ll build 
• Classify programming logs using a hybrid approach 
• Text that has fixed patterns will be classified using regular expression 
• Text that has variable patterns but enough labeled data will be classified using BERT 
and LogisticRegression 
• Text that has complex patterns with very few labeled data points will be classified 
using LLM  
codebasics.io 
 
Tech Stack 
• Core framework: Python, Sklearn, Pandas, Groq

using LLM  
codebasics.io 
 
Tech Stack 
• Core framework: Python, Sklearn, Pandas, Groq 
• Frontend: Streamlit 
• Backend: FastAPI 
• LLM: Deepseek or Qwen

• Additional Dataset: https://www.kaggle.com/search?q=text+classification

5 AI Projects That will Matter in 2026 
 
1. RAG with RBAC, Guardrails and Monitoring 
 
RAG (Retrieval Augmented Generation) is widely used in AI projects worldwide. In our AI 
consultancy company AtliQ, majority of AI implementations we have worked on for our 
clients based in USA and UAE are RAG projects.  
LLMs have knowledge cut-off date, for example GPT 5.4 is trained on data till Aug 2025. If

LLMs have knowledge cut-off date, for example GPT 5.4 is trained on data till Aug 2025. If 
you want to build AI application that uses GPT 5.4 as a brain and reasons over some private 
organizational data or public information post Aug 2025, you will have to use RAG.  
What you will build 
• Build internal chatbot for a company that can answer questions using company’s 
private data 
• Limit the access of information using Role Based Access Control (RBAC) which

private data 
• Limit the access of information using Role Based Access Control (RBAC) which 
means only finance team gets access to financial reports, marketing expenses. Only 
HR team can access employee data and payroll. Only C-level executives have full 
access to all company data 
• Setup guardrails: Protect sensitive / PII data, Out-of-scope question detection 
• Deploy in Azure. Setup monitoring and evaluation so that whenever a new change

• Deploy in Azure. Setup monitoring and evaluation so that whenever a new change 
is deployed it will run evals to make sure things don’t break.  
• Cost monitoring: Setup tracking for token cost, raise alerts when needed

• Core framework: Langchain and/or Docling along with Python  
• Vector Database: Any vector store such as Qdrant, Chromadb or Milvus

• LLM: GPT-OSS or Llama (Use it in groq cloud for free)

• LLM: GPT-OSS or Llama (Use it in groq cloud for free)
